subject: [PATCH v3 04/11] mm: zswap: add range lookup for large-folio swapin

2026-07-03.patch_summary: This patch adds a range lookup feature to the zswap compression algorithm, allowing it to handle large folios by scanning the covered range and checking if any slots are still in zswap. If no slot is found in zswap, the swap_read_folio() function reads from the backing device. The patch also introduces a new function, zswap_range_has_entry(), which enables PMD swap-entry consumers to make the same range decision before attempting PMD-order swapin.

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: Reviewer suggested using zswap_is_present() instead of implementing a new function zswap_range_has_entry(), which can achieve the same result by passing nr=1
reviews.raw_body:
Maybe zswap_is_present() as suggested here:
https://lore.kernel.org/linux-mm/CAO9r8zNni28A+9BiDgDTBRuvGTKnqH91kpbpjjeftfnKSeSOJg@mail.gmail.com/.

IIUC this interface can be used for the same purpose, we just need to
pass nr=1.

2026-07-06.patch_summary: This patch adds a range lookup feature to the zswap compression algorithm, allowing it to efficiently handle large folios (memory blocks) that span multiple pages. The patch introduces a new function `zswap_range_has_entry` that checks if any slot within a given range is present in zswap. This enables zswap to scan the covered range and return an error if any part of the large folio is still stored in zswap, preventing partially initialized data from being returned. The patch also updates the `zswap_load` function to use this new feature.
